questions.question_text: Which activity requires a Montana real estate license?
distractors: Giving free advice to a neighbor
questions.explanation: Negotiating a real estate sale for compensation on behalf of another person requires a Montana real estate license. Selling one's own property, giving free advice, and appraising are not activities requiring a real estate license.

questions.question_text: A Montana investor hires someone to collect rent on 10 rental units. Does the rent collector need a license?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, a property management license is needed
distractors: No, rent collection is exempt from licensing
Only if managing more than 20 rental units
Only a salesperson license is needed
questions.explanation: Under Montana law (Title 37, Chapter 56), property management is a separately licensed activity. Collecting rent for compensation on behalf of another requires a property management license, regardless of the number of units.

questions.question_text: Which person is EXEMPT from Montana real estate licensing requirements?
questions.correct_answer: An owner selling their own property
distractors: A paid referral agent for buyers
A friend negotiating a sale for a fee
A consultant listing homes for commission
questions.explanation: Under MCA 37-51-103, property owners selling their own real estate are exempt from licensing. Persons who negotiate, list, or refer for compensation on behalf of others must hold a license.

questions.question_text: A property owner hires someone to collect rent and manage five residential units for a monthly fee. What license does Montana require?
distractors: No license is needed for rentals
questions.explanation: Montana requires a separate property management license for those who collect rent and manage properties for others. This is distinct from the real estate broker or salesperson license and is governed by a separate statute.

questions.question_text: A homeowner asks a friend who holds no license to list her house for sale and offers a $2,000 fee. Is this lawful?
questions.correct_answer: No, listing for compensation requires a license
distractors: Yes, friends are exempt from licensing rules
Yes, if the fee is under $5,000 total
Only if the friend advertises the property
questions.explanation: Listing real property for sale on behalf of another person for compensation is a licensed activity in Montana regardless of the relationship between the parties. The friend would need a valid real estate license.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum age to obtain a Montana salesperson license?
questions.explanation: Montana requires salesperson license applicants to be at least 18 years of age. This is consistent with many other states' minimum age requirements for real estate licensure.

questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-license education must a Montana salesperson candidate complete?
questions.explanation: Montana requires 70 hours of approved pre-license education for salesperson applicants. This education must be completed before the candidate can sit for the licensing examination.

questions.question_text: A salesperson has been actively licensed for 18 months and wants to become a broker. Does she meet the experience requirement?
questions.correct_answer: No, she needs 2 years of active experience
distractors: Yes, if she completes extra education
No, 3 years of experience is required
questions.explanation: Montana requires a minimum of 2 years of active experience as a licensed salesperson before an individual can apply for a broker license. At 18 months, she has not yet met this requirement.

questions.question_text: A newly licensed salesperson wants to begin practicing immediately. What must she have in addition to passing the exam?
distractors: A business license from the county
questions.explanation: A Montana salesperson must be affiliated with a sponsoring broker before engaging in any real estate activity. The salesperson cannot practice independently; all activity must be under broker supervision.

questions.question_text: What is the minimum education prerequisite for a Montana salesperson applicant?
questions.explanation: Montana requires salesperson applicants to have completed at least 2 years of high school education. This is a lower threshold than many states that require a full diploma or equivalent.

questions.question_text: When must Montana real estate licenses be renewed each year?
questions.explanation: Montana real estate licenses must be renewed annually by October 31. The licensing year runs from November 1 through October 31 of the following year.

questions.question_text: A Montana salesperson fails to renew by October 31. What happens to her license?
distractors: It remains active for 30 more days
questions.explanation: A Montana license that is not renewed by October 31 becomes inactive. The licensee cannot practice until the renewal is completed. Inactive status can be maintained for a limited period before re-examination is required.

questions.question_text: A Montana salesperson completed 8 hours of CE by September. How many more hours are needed before October 31?
questions.explanation: Montana requires 12 hours of continuing education per licensing year, which ends October 31. With 8 hours completed, the salesperson needs 4 more hours to meet the 12-hour annual requirement.

questions.question_text: How many hours of continuing education does Montana require annually for license renewal?
questions.explanation: Montana requires 12 hours of approved continuing education annually for license renewal. Supervising brokers must complete at least 4 of those 12 hours in supervision-related education.

questions.question_text: How long is Montana pre-license education valid before a candidate must pass the exam?
questions.explanation: Montana pre-license education is valid for 24 months from the date of completion. If a candidate does not pass the licensing exam within that window, the education must be retaken.

questions.question_text: A Montana salesperson changes her home address. How quickly must she notify the Board?
questions.explanation: Montana licensees must notify the Board of any address change within 30 days. Failure to provide timely notice may result in disciplinary action.

questions.question_text: A salesperson leaves her sponsoring broker but has not yet affiliated with a new one. What is her license status?
questions.explanation: When a Montana salesperson leaves her sponsoring broker, her license becomes inactive until she affiliates with a new broker. She cannot conduct any real estate activities during this period.

questions.question_text: When a salesperson transfers to a new brokerage in Montana, what must the FORMER broker do?
questions.correct_answer: Return the salesperson's license to the Board
distractors: Transfer the license to the new broker
File a termination report with the MLS
questions.explanation: When a salesperson leaves a brokerage, the former broker must return the salesperson's license to the Board. The new broker then notifies the Board of the new affiliation.

questions.question_text: Where must a Montana licensee's license certificate be kept?
distractors: Kept in the licensee's personal files
questions.explanation: Montana requires that license certificates be displayed at the brokerage office where the licensee is affiliated. This allows consumers and the Board to verify active licensure.

questions.question_text: A broker closes her firm and two salespersons are affiliated with her. What must the broker do with the salesperson licenses?
questions.correct_answer: Return them to the Board promptly
distractors: Give them to the salespersons directly
Hold them until new brokers are found
questions.explanation: When a brokerage closes, the broker is required to return all affiliated salesperson licenses to the Board. The salespersons' licenses become inactive until they affiliate with a new broker.
